task_completion.description: Wondershare EdrawMax is opened and a document with at least two pages is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The EdrawMax application interface is visible, and there are at least two page tabs (e.g., Page-1, Page-2) at the bottom of the canvas.

task_completion.description: Page 1 includes Supplier, Customer, and Production Control boxes with information flow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the first page, there are VSM icons representing a Supplier, a Customer, and a central Production Control box, connected by information flow arrows (often lightning-bolt or dashed arrows).

task_completion.description: Page 1 contains the four specified sequential process boxes with data boxes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four process boxes labeled 'Raw Material Stamping' (or 'Stamping'), 'Welding', 'Surface Treatment', and 'Final Assembly & QC'. Each must have an attached data box (typically a table showing cycle time, changeover time, etc.).

task_completion.description: Page 1 includes inventory triangles, push arrows, and a timeline bar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Triangle icons (representing inventory) between processes, striped or thick push arrows, and a stepped timeline bar at the bottom of the diagram showing value-added and non-value-added times.

task_completion.description: Page 2 illustrates future state improvements with pull/kanban systems and kaizen bursts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the second page, the diagram includes Kanban icons (e.g., cards, posts) or pull arrows, and jagged 'Kaizen burst' shapes highlighting areas for improvement.

task_completion.description: Page 2 contains a summary box detailing the top three improvements and their impact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct text box or table on the second page that lists exactly three improvements along with their expected impacts.

task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The EdrawMax window title bar, tab name, or a save dialog shows the filename 'vsm_current_future.eddx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the EdrawMax GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the EdrawMax application interface, toolbars, and canvas being actively used, rather than terminal commands or scripts generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent used actual EdrawMax VSM shapes rather than pasting a static image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Shapes appear as individual vector elements on the canvas, potentially showing selection handles, alignment guides, or being dragged from the symbol library.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered text into the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Text cursors, highlighted text, or active text boxes are visible within the shapes during the trajectory, proving the text was typed and not just copied as a whole image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The VSM must be a 2-page EdrawMax document saved as '/home/ga/vsm_current_future.eddx'. Page 1 must include 'Stamping', 'Welding', 'Surface Treatment', and 'Final Assembly & QC' process boxes, plus supplier/customer icons, production control, data boxes, inventory triangles, push arrows, and a timeline. Page 2 must include pull/kanban systems, kaizen bursts, and a summary box of 3 improvements.
